And a Supreme Court willing to bless voter suppression, gerrymandering, and a flood of special interest campaign spending is central to making sure we can't win. name: Cory Booker
subject: I'm enormously grateful for you
body:
| | |
---
| Team, as today winds down, I'm wrapping up my fourth month as a candidate for president of the United States. And I want to let you know that I'm enormously grateful for you. We knew from the beginning that we wouldn't be an early frontrunner in this race. We knew others would have a head start -- but that hasn't stopped you from putting your faith in me and this team. Because of you, I'm more optimistic than ever that we can pull off what others say is impossible. Because of you, we've made headway in early states and invested in the important work of building a grassroots infrastructure that won't just help me, but will help Democrats win up and down the ballot next November. Because of you, more people every day are starting to believe in our underdog campaign. All I have to say is THANK YOU. I'll truly never forget the support you've shown me. [_**When we close the books on this month tonight, I hope you 'll be counted as one of our founding donors -- make a donation, even if it's just $1, right now.**_](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70ca/3349388325/VEsE/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190531-em?refcode=em_fr_20190531_CB_20190531-fr-CB-eom3-actives_2020ndnr_l1") Thank you for believing in what we can do together, Cory
---
| | Paid for by Cory 2020
---

P.O. Box 32009 Newark, NJ 07102 | All content (C) 2019 Cory 2020, All Rights Reserved.
Contributions or gifts to Cory 2020 are not tax deductible.
If you got this message from a friend, [you can add your name now to get email updates directly from Cory 2020.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70c9/3349388325/VEsF/ "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter")
This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70c8/3349388325/VEsC/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70d7/3349388325/VEsD/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70d6/3349388325/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70d5/3349388325/VEsB/ "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").
Is this not your preferred email address? You can update your contact info [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0939/5fca0d19/3708b4b/291b70c9/3349388325/VEsO/ "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter").

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: [DEADLINE MIDNIGHT] Keep the momentum going
body:
I want to talk to you about some game changers for our campaign this week. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed29/4133373089/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, before I ask you for help [**_meeting our May fundraising goal before midnight_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed26/4133373089/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190531_JH_20190531-fr-JH-threeupdatesbump-ndnr_ndnr_b1&recurring=auto&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") I want to talk to you about some game changers for our campaign this week:
* **NRA TV went on the attack, criticizing my calls for a national gun licensing standard.** The NRA is lashing out because they know we can beat them, like we already have in Colorado--passing universal background checks and high-capacity magazine limits. (My message for them: We're not going anywhere, and we won't stand down.)

* **I released my plan to protect women 's reproductive rights across our nation** by making Colorado's successful long-acting reversible contraception (LARC) program national. This would protect the rights of all women to control their own healthcare and bodies by providing access to affordable contraceptive options such as IUDs. (In Colorado, the results spoke for themselves: We saw a 64% drop in teenage abortion, a 54% drop in teen pregnancy, _and_ the program saved taxpayers $70 million.)

* **I went on the record calling for impeachment inquiry:** Robert Mueller's press conference on Wednesday convinced me that it's time for Congress to fulfill its constitutional duty to further the investigation.

Week by week, we're growing this campaign and getting our message out, everywhere from local meet and greets to national outlets like CNN and MSNBC. Our vision of an America united behind smart, humane, progressive policies is gaining traction.

**But one more game changer hit our campaign this week. The DNC doubled the number of individual grassroots donors it will take to qualify for the later debates: from 65,000 to 130,000.**

Hitting that goal and taking our message to the debate stage to speak up for gun safety, reproductive rights, and more will take _everyone_ on this team stepping up. The finance team tells me we're still over $20,000 from the goal we needed to hit by tonight, so I'm here to personally ask you:

**Before tonight 's deadline, will you consider making your first gift to our campaign? Just $1 is all it takes to help grow this team:** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed26/4133373089/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190531_JH_20190531-fr-JH-threeupdatesbump-ndnr_ndnr_b1&recurring=auto&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed24/4133373089/VEsD/ **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed25/4133373089/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190531_JH_20190531-fr-JH-threeupdatesbump-ndnr_ndnr_b50&recurring=auto&amount=50&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed22/4133373089/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190531_JH_20190531-fr-JH-threeupdatesbump-ndnr_ndnr_b100&recurring=auto&amount=100&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed23/4133373089/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190531_JH_20190531-fr-JH-threeupdatesbump-ndnr_ndnr_bother&recurring=auto&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** I'm so grateful you're on this team and going on this journey with me.

Thank you,
John [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed20/4133373089/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190531_JH_20190531-fr-JH-threeupdatesbump-ndnr_ndnr_contribbutton&recurring=auto&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Keep us company on the campaign trail by joining us on [_**Facebook**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed5e/4133373089/VEsHBA/ "https://www.facebook.com/JohnHickenlooper/"), [_**Twitter**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed5f/4133373089/VEsHBw/ "https://twitter.com/hickenlooper"), [_**Instagram**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed5c/4133373089/VEsHBg/ "https://www.instagram.com/johnhickenlooper/"), and [_**YouTube**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771609/691c9bd/6c25570d/11a4ed5d/4133373089/VEsHAQ/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC82O0Byp_ny13O0LkO1UpUg/featured").

name: Tennessee Democratic Party
subject: Three Star Dinner Update, Elevate Summit 2019 Recap, Caucuses, and a Podcast
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
| | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/4IfLjRt4oCRpHlIHQ6_UgRTOBnHdZNuPsPC3M2D_W7Y=) Dear Peter, The **Tennessee Democratic Party 2019 THREE STAR DINNER** is just three weeks away! **** The TNDP Three Star Dinner is the premiere yearly Tennessee Democratic Party rally and fundraising event and brings together Democrats from across the state, Mountain City to Memphis. The importance of this night cannot be overstated as it allows us to celebrate our successes **TOGETHER** and raise the dollars necessary to continue the important work of revitalizing our Party from the ground up and **WINNING ELECTIONS**! Please join us at this year's event as we welcome **[U.S. SENATOR KYRSTEN SINEMA](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/1D0VzLc02kMUdWXmPZuJlpZtGmgJZ9fvgvfH2yss6z4=)** as our keynote speaker and [**CONGRESSMAN JIM COOPER**](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/n9jqxGllGaPOYOOtSMuHZQLOtO4-jpcEeJCDMFVeiGQ=) as our special guest. Details are below and more information, as well as tickets & sponsorships, can be found at **[tndp.org/ThreeStar](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/Co6BzkSLO28CD8o_1w9Dv8Lh2ZQ2LXFjQnjB1ilDVFs=)** **TNDP THREE STAR DINNER** Saturday, June 22, 2019 6:00 - 8:00 pm CT **Music City Center** 201 Fifth Avenue South Nashville, TN 37203 **TICKETS** Individual Ticket - $150 Student/Child Ticket - $50 [**Buy Tickers Here**](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/Co6BzkSLO28CD8o_1w9Dv8Lh2ZQ2LXFjQnjB1ilDVFs=) **SPONSORSHIPS** Victory Sponsor - $50,000 Courage Sponsor - $25,000 Unity Sponsor - $10,000 Vision Sponsor - $5,000 Freedom Sponsor - $2,500 Grassroots Sponsor - $1,500 [**Purchase Sponsorships Here**](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/Co6BzkSLO28CD8o_1w9Dv8Lh2ZQ2LXFjQnjB1ilDVFs=) **PROGRAM ADS** Full Page - $500 Half Page - $300 Quarter Page - $200 Business Card - $100 **[Purchase Program Advertisements Here](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/Co6BzkSLO28CD8o_1w9Dv8Lh2ZQ2LXFjQnjB1ilDVFs=)** **TNDP ELEVATE SUMMIT RECAP** Thank you to the organizers, presenters, and speakers who helped make the TNDP Elevate Summit 2019 successful and beneficial for all who attended. We conducted over 20 workshops for Democratic organizers and county party leaders, held the inaugural founding meetings for six [Tennessee Democratic Party Caucuses](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/7l2XDzgcxWa_m8keeu-uu77hrJHnNJGyYcTkzMyTNEw=), and enjoyed a word from 2020 U.S. Senate candidate[ James Mackler](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/35ZQo06slRNV-OlQUI6RPqwQcwFHS9KT2XA9Dr9HMnY=) and a remarkable keynote speech from 2020 Democratic Presidential candidate [Julian Castro](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/4bn6zs_CAB7SsW1opDpYSjj6IsOXQh9fccpnjZZLCzs=). If you were unable to join us, no worries -- Julian offers a solid recap of the points he made in his keynote address and more in the latest episode of the **[LEAD 2020 TNDP Podcast](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/sQTogvB6WtcxIfGH9YQH6QGzVAD3qpkpgDZcp0eFI2M=?i=1000439175349)**. The Elevate Summit 2019 marked the launched the following TNDP membership caucuses: African-American Caucus, Hispanic Caucus, Rural Caucus, Veterans & Military Families Caucus, and the LTGBTQ+ Caucus. If you share an experience with any of these groups, we encourage you to visit our [**Caucuses page**](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/7l2XDzgcxWa_m8keeu-uu77hrJHnNJGyYcTkzMyTNEw=) to join or find out how to form a new caucus. **LEAD 2020 PODCAST** The LEAD 2020 is connecting Democrats around the state with leaders at every level and in every corner of the Democratic Party. Listen as they provide the know-how, inspiration, and vision to help move our Party and country forward. In May, LEAD 2020 guests included:
* **Julian Castro** : Former Obama HUD Secretary, Former San Antonio mayor, TNDP Elevate Summit Keynote Speaker, and 2020 Democratic Presidential hopeful. This episode is available **[here](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/sQTogvB6WtcxIfGH9YQH6QGzVAD3qpkpgDZcp0eFI2M=?i=1000439175349)**.
* **Matt Hildreth** : Director of New Initiatives at America's Voice and founder and Board-Chair of RuralOrganizing.org. Matt's episode, which addresses how we can empower rural Democrats and create thriving and equitable rural communities, can be found **[here](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/I1fmv5Klm0Wm0B7PaX-bbHlrdNjjkgLS4LHUkE8tNco=?i=1000438014732)**.
* **James Mackler** : Former prosecutor, Army veteran, Tennessee Air National Guard JAG Officer, and 2020 Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate. Check out Jame's episode [**here**](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/TWdRFVFhsnY3SqWjeTYlWrNJoMshB1PZcRYvz-FFuAU=?i=1000437180713).

Don't forget to subscribe to the TNDP LEAD 2020 Podcast on Apple Podcasts and Spotify! Take good care and looking forward to seeing you on June 22! Mary
\--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party **HELP US AMPLIFY OUR MESSAGE** We can always use the help of dedicated Democrats like you to help us amplify our message. It's easy, just click on the links below and amplify us! Tennessee Democratic Party [Twitter](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/ZS8nMNIByF4G5kerqRkExpHNZp0dT9Mx0Khcq6YsDhA=) | [Facebook](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/s8hPPJU9A_JBggpS69STaGXKAOCoyqQxTwjVxoVWsZY=) | [Instagram](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/K6sITZeRmHehxTvSMDS4tndETC4mJiEEBppHT2t_voI=) | Subscribe to [LEAD 2020 Podcast](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/tjqFwySIQidbAzPZlxaa654uQhB-wHawGfuawiXbEJ0=) Chair Mary Mancini [Twitter](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/0l75ArbZHTdnrvG6plpRQNPGynzM2FS9A9m5gDkkC88=) | [Facebook](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/4Pk2gSYZr91s1Ho9sBEd4aFij77hhfAz_kESy7vc2qc=) | [Instagram](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/Wh1EPbnScy2Rd2lubS5yerrihvM58YoHs0JFDSIM1Ic=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/d-A0CKaCaTTHoqihPHVdoYDObpmiLzWKk19iAX-hnUY=/GsfmMFJjQKkOuWpD9ozttAfUcoz5ovO0-S7_FLWiZwU=)

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
"The president showered the U.S. Air Force Academy’s graduating class with praise and encouragement as they are ‘ready to fly, fight, and win.'" | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8f37253a2b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [Trump Shakes ‘Every Single Hand’ of US Air Force Graduates While Praising Their ‘Noble Road of Service’](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5f44bb578a&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Independent Journal Review_
“The president showered the U.S. Air Force Academy’s graduating class with praise and encouragement as they are ‘ready to fly, fight, and win,’” Madison Summers reports. “During the ceremony, Trump announced that he would shake ‘every single hand’ of the nearly [1,000] graduates as they walk across the stage.”

🎬**Watch:** [President Trump delivers 2019 Air Force Academy commencement speech](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ae5402a6df&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [Over 1,000 Illegal Immigrants Apprehended in El Paso Sector, Largest Group Ever at Single Time](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bae033e5e4&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“More than 1,000 illegal immigrants were apprehended by U.S. Customs and Border Protection agents near the U.S.-Mexico border early Wednesday—the largest ever group of migrants ever apprehended at a single time,” John Roberts and Travis Fedschun report. “The fact is that [the Rio Grande Valley] is receiving caravan-equivalent numbers every seven days,” Sector Chief Patrol Agent Rodolfo Karisch said.
---
|
---
| [Why Trump’s Tariffs on Mexico are Needed to Cut Illegal Immigration](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b86a025d7f&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“President Trump sent a message of strength and leadership Thursday night when he announced he will impose escalating tariffs on Mexico in a bold move to halt the flow of illegal immigrants and protect our national security,” National Border Patrol Council President Brandon Judd writes. “The congressional inaction we are now witnessing is made worse by Mexico’s refusal to enforce its own immigration laws, which are actually much stronger than our own.”
---
|
---
| [Democrats Should Forget Impeachment and Turn the Page](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3e5e2c0403&e=3ee4985eea)
-CNN
“It would be divisive for the nation and a boon to our global competitors if Democrats choose to hobble our national agenda with superfluous, partisan impeachment,” Carrie Sheffield writes. “Clearly, Mueller is moving on and it's time for us to move on as a country, too . . . By a nearly 20-point margin, the American people do not want impeachment, according to polling by the Washington Post and ABC News.”
---
|
---
| **[My Fellow Democrats Are Skeptical About the USMCA. They Shouldn't Be](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9d257b72b9&e=3ee4985eea)**
_-Fortune_
“Congress will soon vote on the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), a landmark trade pact finalized by all three nations last November,” former Democratic Governor of Washington Gary Locke writes. USMCA is a “historic win for workers,” Locke says. “It would guarantee fairer pay through wage requirements, ensure safe working environments, and strengthen workers’ rights to unionize for better benefits.”
---
|
---
| [Trump Trip to Japan Will Benefit US and Our Allies](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c90be0b09e&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“President Trump’s just-concluded visit to Japan was more than ceremonial,” Brett Velicovich writes. “By strengthening our relationship with steadfast allies such as Japan, the president has placed America in an even better position to pacify the North Korean regime and bring an end to one of the biggest global security challenges of the century.”

name: Andrew Yang
subject: Pride Month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=06012019_newsletter_2&n=1)[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=06012019_newsletter_2&n=2) This year marks the 50th Anniversary of the Stonewall Riots. In some ways, we as a country have made great strides in providing equal rights to LGBTQ+ individuals, highlighted by the decision in Obergefell in 2015. That Supreme Court decision had a tangible, positive impact on the lives of millions of Americans who were looking to receive equal recognition from their government for their families. However, we can do a lot better at implementing policies that would make a difference in the lives of LGBTQ+ Americans. This is one of the reasons I think the Freedom Dividend of $1,000 a month is the best policy for advancing our country. A recent survey of LGBTQ+ individuals between the ages of 18 and 25 found that 1 out of 10 had experienced homelessness within the previous 12 months. This is driven in large part by losing support from their families simply for being who they are. The Freedom Dividend would provide these individuals with resources in order to start building their lives by moving to a new area or finding housing. Additionally, passing a Medicare for All law with strong protections and coverage for all Americans would be especially beneficial to the LGBTQ+ community. This group often faces issues getting health insurance, or receiving treatment without facing discrimination. This is especially true for mental health issues, which are faced at triple the rate seen in the rest of the population. Under my presidency, everyone would have access to quality care that would address all issues without judgment or discrimination. Everyone would also be free to serve in the military during my administration. Many generals and admirals agree that the current administration's ban on transgender military service harms combat effectiveness and compromises the military's values. I would be honored and humbled to serve as Commander in Chief to any patriot who is willing to sacrifice everything in order to protect our country. Every American should be free to live the life that they want to live, without fear of homelessness, or being denied quality medical care, or being denied the honor of serving in our military. We can do better for LGBTQ+ Americans and all Americans. We are all human, love is love, and people are people. -Andrew
---

Dear Friend

This morning I joined Joy Reid on MSNBC’s AM Joy to discuss Georgia’s abortion ban and the ripple effect of negative consequences we’re already seeing as businesses reconsider their investment in states that have passed these abhorrent bans.

“This is political malpractice. No one should run a state like this and put the lives of women in Georgia at risk.” [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/msnbc-morning-joy-060119?refcode=060119]

As Senator, I will stand up for women across America. My message is simple: Trust women. Stop the bans. And that is the case I made on AM Joy this morning.

Watch the clip from MSNBC’s AM Joy here:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/msnbc-morning-joy-060119?refcode=060119]

As I explained on AM Joy: “ This is a Republican strategy to gin up their base. They’re using women as pawns in their game - and that’s exactly what’s going on: they’re trying to get Trump re-elected and Republicans re-elected when they know they have no record to run on. We won’t be played. "

If Republican elected officials cannot trust women to make these personal medical decisions, we cannot trust them in elected office.
“This law, particularly the one in Georgia, and now in 11 other states, relegates pregnant women to be vessels of the state, requiring them to carry pregnancies to term. It also says women are incompetent to decide their own medical decisions. And that’s just repugnant to so many people.”

People who grew up where I did...

Who were raised by single moms like I was...

Who come from a family of immigrants like I do...

We're not supposed to run for President -- and we're certainly not supposed to be frontrunners.

**But I want kids from all walks of life to know that -- no matter where you come from -- you can be President, too.**

Which is why sharing my story and vision in every Democratic debate is so personally important to me.

[_**But it was just announced that the threshold to qualify for future debates is going to be even tougher than the first two. Will you take my quick poll if you think I deserve to share my unique story on that national stage? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdca/3eb3e2fc/414d03d7/7449789c/2587444965/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190601_em_pet_b1_castro_all_rs")

name: Dan Baer
subject: about LGBTQ+ Pride Month:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
I would love to hear your stories about Pride.
|
---

| | Before I do anything else, I want to say thank you. Your generous support last month is why we knocked our End of Month goal out of the park, and I could not be more grateful. We're building a larger grassroots campaign every day thanks to people like you. In other news, today marks the beginning of Pride! |
---

In today's seemingly endless fountain of discouraging news, it's a chance to take a step back and look at how far we've come.

**So I wanted to take some time to talk about what Pride means to me.**

This month, we celebrate the historic progress for LGBTQ+ individuals in our country, progress driven by our community, often working with allies, and by all those who marched, protested and worked over many decades to dismantle hateful legislation and barriers: from the very early gay civil rights movement, to the Stonewall Riots, to _Obergefell v. Hodges_. The project of advancing civil and human rights is never the work of one person, or one group of people alone: it's a project for coalitions across time and space.

While I was growing up here in Colorado, an anti-LGBTQ+ amendment passed that prohibited protections for LGBTQ+ individuals. Back then, Colorado was labeled "The Hate State."

**Look how far we 've come in only 20 years.**

Marriage equality is the law of the land across the country, the Democratic House just passed the Equality Act ensuring protections for members of the LGBTQ+ community, and an openly gay man is running for President.

There's more work to do, to be sure. But my younger self never imagined a future where I'd have had the opportunity to serve our country as an ambassador overseas while being happily married to my husband Brian -- let alone a future where I could be the first openly gay man to serve in the U.S. Senate.

**So, what does Pride mean to you?**

If you're willing, I would love to hear your stories about Pride, whether it's something that has taught you about the value of community, a special memory, what you've learned about how to be an ally, anything at all that's meaningful to you.

Submit your stories at the link below, and we'll select a few people and stories to share on my [Facebook](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82622/232dfd1/94587cc/62a17f45/3553622400/VEsH/ "http://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/"), [Instagram](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82622/232dfd1/94587cc/62a17f4a/3553622400/VEsE/ "http://www.instagram.com/danforcolorado/"), and [Twitter](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82622/232dfd1/94587cc/62a17f4b/3553622400/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/danbbaer") accounts in the coming month.

| [**TAKE THE SURVEY >> **](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82622/232dfd1/94587cc/62a17f48/3553622400/VEsC/ "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/pride-survey-personal?source=20190601_em_pet_b1_baer_all")

Thank you, and happy Pride!

Dan

Your generous support last month is why we knocked our End of Month goal out of the park, and I could not be more grateful. We're building a larger grassroots campaign every day thanks to people like you. In other news, today marks the beginning of Pride! | In today's seemingly endless fountain of discouraging news, it's a chance to take a step back and look at how far we've come. So I wanted to take some time to talk about what Pride means to me. This month, we celebrate the historic progress for LGBTQ+ individuals in our country, progress driven by our community, often working with allies, and by all those who marched, protested and worked over many decades to dismantle hateful legislation and barriers: from the very early gay civil rights movement, to the Stonewall Riots, to Obergefell v. Hodges. The project of advancing civil and human rights is never the work of one person, or one group of people alone: it's a project for coalitions across time and space. While I was growing up here in Colorado, an anti-LGBTQ+ amendment passed that prohibited protections for LGBTQ+ individuals. Back then, Colorado was labeled "The Hate State." Look how far we 've come in only 20 years. Marriage equality is the law of the land across the country, the Democratic House just passed the Equality Act ensuring protections for members of the LGBTQ+ community, and an openly gay man is running for President. Will you sign our petition demanding Mitch McConnell hold a vote on the Equality Act? | [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319418/1cd0ac28/234971bc/356c936c/889979676/VEsH/ "https://www.joebiden.com")
---
Peter,

Happy Pride Month! Joe is in Columbus, Ohio today to give a speech reflecting on the work we still need to do to fight for LGBTQ equality. **[Before Joe takes the stage tonight, we wanted to ask you to join Joe in calling on the Senate to pass the Equality Act.](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319418/1cd0ac28/234971bc/356c936b/889979676/VEsE/ "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/equality-act-june?source=email-bsd-20190601equalityact-b1_PET_ac_NA-F1_S1_C1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=20190601equalityact&utm_content=b1_PET_ac_NA&utm_term=F1_S1_C1")**

**It 's pretty simple:** No one should live in fear of being fired, evicted from their home, or denied service in a restaurant just because of who they are or who they love.

That's why we were so happy when the House of Representatives passed the long overdue Equality Act.

But in order for this to become the law of the land, Mitch McConnell has to bring it up for a vote in the Senate -- which he so far has refused to do.

Peter, every day he waits is another day LGBTQ Americans live as second class citizens.

**So will you sign our petition demanding Mitch McConnell hold a vote on the Equality Act?** | [**SIGN THE PETITION ->**](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319418/1cd0ac28/234971bc/356c936b/889979676/VEsF/ "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/equality-act-june?source=email-bsd-20190601equalityact-b1_PET_ac_NA-F1_S1_C1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=20190601equalityact&utm_content=b1_PET_ac_NA&utm_term=F1_S1_C1")
---
Winning the presidency is incredibly important, but until we can put a new Administration in the White House, we need to do everything we can to protect the rights of all people.

**That starts with making sure we can get a law passed that prohibits discrimination as soon as possible. Joe will continue speaking out on this issue and we hope you will too, Peter.**

**[Will you join us in using your voice to demand that Mitch McConnell holds a vote on the Equality Act in the Senate so we can pass it once and for all? You can do that here:](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319418/1cd0ac28/234971bc/356c936b/889979676/VEsC/ "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/equality-act-june?source=email-bsd-20190601equalityact-b1_PET_ac_NA-F1_S1_C1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=20190601equalityact&utm_content=b1_PET_ac_NA&utm_term=F1_S1_C1")**
